explanations.8: This checks if the requested file doesn't actually exist on disk (the !-f means 'not a file'), so we can send non-existent requests to index.html.
explanations.9: This rewrites all requests to index.html while preserving query parameters (QSA) and stopping any further rewrites (L means 'last rule').
explanations.26: This tells Apache to enable SSL/TLS encryption for this virtual host running on port 443.
explanations.28: This points Apache to the certificate file that proves your server's identity to browsers.
explanations.30: This is the private key that matches your certificate—keep this secure, as it's needed to decrypt HTTPS traffic.
